reason: Adding the 'nosuid' mount option to the EFI partition is a standard security hardening practice to prevent setuid binaries from executing from that partition. It is a non-disruptive change to /etc/fstab that does not affect the ability to boot or access the system, provided no legitimate setuid binaries are required to reside in /boot/efi (which is standard for RHEL/Rocky).
estimated_impact: reboot required; filesystems affected; Potential for failure if /boot/efi is not explicitly defined in /etc/fstab
